NKJV.1: Now I saw a new heaven and a new earth, for the first heaven and the first earth had passed away. Also there was no more sea.
NKJV.2: Then I, John, saw the holy city, New Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, prepared as a bride adorned for her husband.
NKJV.3: And I heard a loud voice from heaven saying, “Behold, the tabernacle of God is with men, and He will dwell with them, and they shall be His people. God Himself will be with them and be their God.
NKJV.4: And God will wipe away every tear from their eyes; there shall be no more death, nor sorrow, nor crying. There shall be no more pain, for the former things have passed away.”
NKJV.5: Then He who sat on the throne said, “Behold, I make all things new.” And He said to me, “Write, for these words are true and faithful.”
NKJV.6: And He said to me, “It is done! I am the Alpha and the Omega, the Beginning and the End. I will give of the fountain of the water of life freely to him who thirsts.
NKJV.7: He who overcomes shall inherit all things, and I will be his God and he shall be My son.
NKJV.8: But the cowardly, unbelieving, abominable, murderers, sexually immoral, sorcerers, idolaters, and all liars shall have their part in the lake which burns with fire and brimstone, which is the second death.”
NKJV.9: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls filled with the seven last plagues came to me and talked with me, saying, “Come, I will show you the bride, the Lamb’s wife.”
NKJV.10: And he carried me away in the Spirit to a great and high mountain, and showed me the great city, the holy Jerusalem, descending out of heaven from God,
NKJV.11: having the glory of God. Her light was like a most precious stone, like a jasper stone, clear as crystal.
NKJV.12: Also she had a great and high wall with twelve gates, and twelve angels at the gates, and names written on them, which are the names of the twelve tribes of the children of Israel:
NKJV.13: three gates on the east, three gates on the north, three gates on the south, and three gates on the west.
NKJV.14: Now the wall of the city had twelve foundations, and on them were the names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.
NKJV.15: And he who talked with me had a gold reed to measure the city, its gates, and its wall.
NKJV.16: The city is laid out as a square; its length is as great as its breadth. And he measured the city with the reed: twelve thousand furlongs. Its length, breadth, and height are equal.
NKJV.17: Then he measured its wall: one hundred and forty-four cubits, according to the measure of a man, that is, of an angel.
NKJV.18: The construction of its wall was of jasper; and the city was pure gold, like clear glass.
NKJV.19: The foundations of the wall of the city were adorned with all kinds of precious stones: the first foundation was jasper, the second sapphire, the third chalcedony, the fourth emerald,
NKJV.20: the fifth sardonyx, the sixth sardius, the seventh chrysolite, the eighth beryl, the ninth topaz, the tenth chrysoprase, the eleventh jacinth, and the twelfth amethyst.
NKJV.21: The twelve gates were twelve pearls: each individual gate was of one pearl. And the street of the city was pure gold, like transparent glass.
NKJV.22: But I saw no temple in it, for the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb are its temple.
NKJV.23: The city had no need of the sun or of the moon to shine in it, for the glory of God illuminated it. The Lamb is its light.
NKJV.24: And the nations of those who are saved shall walk in its light, and the kings of the earth bring their glory and honor into it.
NKJV.25: Its gates shall not be shut at all by day (there shall be no night there).
NKJV.26: And they shall bring the glory and the honor of the nations into it.
NKJV.27: But there shall by no means enter it anything that defiles, or causes an abomination or a lie, but only those who are written in the Lamb’s Book of Life.

ESV.1: Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth, for the first heaven and the first earth had passed away, and the sea was no more.
ESV.2: And I saw the holy city, new Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, prepared as a bride adorned for her husband.
ESV.3: And I heard a loud voice from the throne saying, “Behold, the dwelling place  of God is with man. He will dwell with them, and they will be his people,  and God himself will be with them as their God.
ESV.4: He will wipe away every tear from their eyes, and death shall be no more, neither shall there be mourning, nor crying, nor pain anymore, for the former things have passed away.”
ESV.5: And he who was seated on the throne said, “Behold, I am making all things new.” Also he said, “Write this down, for these words are trustworthy and true.”
ESV.6: And he said to me, “It is done! I am the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. To the thirsty I will give from the spring of the water of life without payment.
ESV.7: The one who conquers will have this heritage, and I will be his God and he will be my son.
ESV.8: But as for the cowardly, the faithless, the detestable, as for murderers, the sexually immoral, sorcerers, idolaters, and all liars, their portion will be in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur, which is the second death.”
ESV.9: Then came one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls full of the seven last plagues and spoke to me, saying, “Come, I will show you the Bride, the wife of the Lamb.”
ESV.10: And he carried me away in the Spirit to a great, high mountain, and showed me the holy city Jerusalem coming down out of heaven from God,
ESV.11: having the glory of God, its radiance like a most rare jewel, like a jasper, clear as crystal.
ESV.12: It had a great, high wall, with twelve gates, and at the gates twelve angels, and on the gates the names of the twelve tribes of the sons of Israel were inscribed —
ESV.13: on the east three gates, on the north three gates, on the south three gates, and on the west three gates.
ESV.14: And the wall of the city had twelve foundations, and on them were the twelve names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.
ESV.15: And the one who spoke with me had a measuring rod of gold to measure the city and its gates and walls.
ESV.16: The city lies foursquare, its length the same as its width. And he measured the city with his rod, 12,000 stadia.  Its length and width and height are equal.
ESV.17: He also measured its wall, 144 cubits  by human measurement, which is also an angel's measurement.
ESV.18: The wall was built of jasper, while the city was pure gold, like clear glass.
ESV.19: The foundations of the wall of the city were adorned with every kind of jewel. The first was jasper, the second sapphire, the third agate, the fourth emerald,
ESV.20: the fifth onyx, the sixth carnelian, the seventh chrysolite, the eighth beryl, the ninth topaz, the tenth chrysoprase, the eleventh jacinth, the twelfth amethyst.
ESV.21: And the twelve gates were twelve pearls, each of the gates made of a single pearl, and the street of the city was pure gold, like transparent glass.
ESV.22: And I saw no temple in the city, for its temple is the Lord God the Almighty and the Lamb.
ESV.23: And the city has no need of sun or moon to shine on it, for the glory of God gives it light, and its lamp is the Lamb.
ESV.24: By its light will the nations walk, and the kings of the earth will bring their glory into it,
ESV.25: and its gates will never be shut by day — and there will be no night there.
ESV.26: They will bring into it the glory and the honor of the nations.
ESV.27: But nothing unclean will ever enter it, nor anyone who does what is detestable or false, but only those who are written in the Lamb's book of life.

NASB.1: Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth; for the first heaven and the first earth passed away, and there is no longer [any] sea.
NASB.2: And I saw the holy city, new Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, made ready as a bride adorned for her husband.
NASB.3: And I heard a loud voice from the throne, saying, 'Behold, the tabernacle of God is among men, and He will dwell among them, and they shall be His people, and God Himself will be among them,
NASB.4: and He will wipe away every tear from their eyes; and there will no longer be [any] death; there will no longer be [any] mourning, or crying, or pain; the first things have passed away.'
NASB.5: And He who sits on the throne said, 'Behold, I am making all things new.' And He said, 'Write, for these words are faithful and true.'
NASB.6: Then He said to me, 'It is done. I am the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. I will give to the one who thirsts from the spring of the water of life without cost.
NASB.7: 'He who overcomes will inherit these things, and I will be his God and he will be My son.
NASB.8: 'But for the cowardly and unbelieving and abominable and murderers and immoral persons and sorcerers and idolaters and all liars, their part [will be] in the lake that burns with fire and brimstone, which is the second death.'
NASB.9: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls full of the seven last plagues came and spoke with me, saying, 'Come here, I will show you the bride, the wife of the Lamb.'
NASB.10: And he carried me away in the Spirit to a great and high mountain, and showed me the holy city, Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God,
NASB.11: having the glory of God. Her brilliance was like a very costly stone, as a stone of crystal-clear jasper.
NASB.12: It had a great and high wall, with twelve gates, and at the gates twelve angels; and names [were] written on them, which are [the names] of the twelve tribes of the sons of Israel.
NASB.13: [There were] three gates on the east and three gates on the north and three gates on the south and three gates on the west.
NASB.14: And the wall of the city had twelve foundation stones, and on them [were] the twelve names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.
NASB.15: The one who spoke with me had a gold measuring rod to measure the city, and its gates and its wall.
NASB.16: The city is laid out as a square, and its length is as great as the width; and he measured the city with the rod, fifteen hundred miles; its length and width and height are equal.
NASB.17: And he measured its wall, seventy-two yards, [according to] human measurements, which are [also] angelic [measurements].
NASB.18: The material of the wall was jasper; and the city was pure gold, like clear glass.
NASB.19: The foundation stones of the city wall were adorned with every kind of precious stone. The first foundation stone was jasper; the second, sapphire; the third, chalcedony; the fourth, emerald;
NASB.20: the fifth, sardonyx; the sixth, sardius; the seventh, chrysolite; the eighth, beryl; the ninth, topaz; the tenth, chrysoprase; the eleventh, jacinth; the twelfth, amethyst.
NASB.21: And the twelve gates were twelve pearls; each one of the gates was a single pearl. And the street of the city was pure gold, like transparent glass.
NASB.22: I saw no temple in it, for the Lord God the Almighty and the Lamb are its temple.
NASB.23: And the city has no need of the sun or of the moon to shine on it, for the glory of God has illumined it, and its lamp [is] the Lamb.
NASB.24: The nations will walk by its light, and the kings of the earth will bring their glory into it.
NASB.25: In the daytime (for there will be no night there) its gates will never be closed;
NASB.26: and they will bring the glory and the honor of the nations into it;
NASB.27: and nothing unclean, and no one who practices abomination and lying, shall ever come into it, but only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life.

NLT.1: Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth, for the old heaven and the old earth had disappeared. And the sea was also gone.
NLT.2: And I saw the holy city, the new Jerusalem, coming down from God out of heaven like a bride beautifully dressed for her husband.
NLT.3: I heard a loud shout from the throne, saying, “Look, God’s home is now among his people! He will live with them, and they will be his people. God himself will be with them.
NLT.4: He will wipe every tear from their eyes, and there will be no more death or sorrow or crying or pain. All these things are gone forever.”
NLT.5: And the one sitting on the throne said, “Look, I am making everything new!” And then he said to me, “Write this down, for what I tell you is trustworthy and true.”
NLT.6: And he also said, “It is finished! I am the Alpha and the Omega — the Beginning and the End. To all who are thirsty I will give freely from the springs of the water of life.
NLT.7: All who are victorious will inherit all these blessings, and I will be their God, and they will be my children.
NLT.8: “But cowards, unbelievers, the corrupt, murderers, the immoral, those who practice witchcraft, idol worshipers, and all liars — their fate is in the fiery lake of burning sulfur. This is the second death.”
NLT.9: Then one of the seven angels who held the seven bowls containing the seven last plagues came and said to me, “Come with me! I will show you the bride, the wife of the Lamb.”
NLT.10: So he took me in the Spirit to a great, high mountain, and he showed me the holy city, Jerusalem, descending out of heaven from God.
NLT.11: It shone with the glory of God and sparkled like a precious stone — like jasper as clear as crystal.
NLT.12: The city wall was broad and high, with twelve gates guarded by twelve angels. And the names of the twelve tribes of Israel were written on the gates.
NLT.13: There were three gates on each side — east, north, south, and west.
NLT.14: The wall of the city had twelve foundation stones, and on them were written the names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.
NLT.15: The angel who talked to me held in his hand a gold measuring stick to measure the city, its gates, and its wall.
NLT.16: When he measured it, he found it was a square, as wide as it was long. In fact, its length and width and height were each 1,400 miles.
NLT.17: Then he measured the walls and found them to be 216 feet thick (according to the human standard used by the angel).
NLT.18: The wall was made of jasper, and the city was pure gold, as clear as glass.
NLT.19: The wall of the city was built on foundation stones inlaid with twelve precious stones: the first was jasper, the second sapphire, the third agate, the fourth emerald,
NLT.20: the fifth onyx, the sixth carnelian, the seventh chrysolite, the eighth beryl, the ninth topaz, the tenth chrysoprase, the eleventh jacinth, the twelfth amethyst.
NLT.21: The twelve gates were made of pearls — each gate from a single pearl! And the main street was pure gold, as clear as glass.
NLT.22: I saw no temple in the city, for the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb are its temple.
NLT.23: And the city has no need of sun or moon, for the glory of God illuminates the city, and the Lamb is its light.
NLT.24: The nations will walk in its light, and the kings of the world will enter the city in all their glory.
NLT.25: Its gates will never be closed at the end of day because there is no night there.
NLT.26: And all the nations will bring their glory and honor into the city.
NLT.27: Nothing evil will be allowed to enter, nor anyone who practices shameful idolatry and dishonesty — but only those whose names are written in the Lamb’s Book of Life.

WEB.1: I saw a new heaven and a new earth: for the first heaven and the first earth have passed away, and the sea is no more.
WEB.2: I saw the holy city, New Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, prepared like a bride adorned for her husband.
WEB.3: I heard a loud voice out of heaven saying, “Behold, God’s dwelling is with people, and he will dwell with them, and they will be his people, and God himself will be with them as their God.
WEB.4: He will wipe away from them every tear from their eyes. Death will be no more; neither will there be mourning, nor crying, nor pain, any more. The first things have passed away.”
WEB.5: He who sits on the throne said, “Behold, I am making all things new.” He said, “Write, for these words of God are faithful and true.”
WEB.6: He said to me, “I have become the Alpha and the Omega, the Beginning and the End.  I will give freely to him who is thirsty from the spring of the water of life.
WEB.7: He who overcomes, I will give him these things. I will be his God, and he will be my son.
WEB.8: But for the cowardly, unbelieving, sinners, abominable, murderers, sexually immoral, sorcerers,  idolaters, and all liars, their part is in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur, which is the second death.”
WEB.9: One of the seven angels who had the seven bowls, who were loaded with the seven last plagues came, and he spoke with me, saying, “Come here. I will show you the wife, the Lamb’s bride.”
WEB.10: He carried me away in the Spirit to a great and high mountain, and showed me the holy city, Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God,
WEB.11: having the glory of God. Her light was like a most precious stone, as if it were a jasper stone, clear as crystal;
WEB.12: having a great and high wall; having twelve gates, and at the gates twelve angels; and names written on them, which are the names of the twelve tribes of the children of Israel.
WEB.13: On the east were three gates; and on the north three gates; and on the south three gates; and on the west three gates.
WEB.14: The wall of the city had twelve foundations, and on them twelve names of the twelve Apostles of the Lamb.
WEB.15: He who spoke with me had for a measure a golden reed to measure the city, its gates, and its walls.
WEB.16: The city is square, and its length is as great as its width. He measured the city with the reed, twelve thousand twelve stadia.  Its length, width, and height are equal.
WEB.17: Its wall is one hundred forty-four cubits,  by the measure of a man, that is, of an angel.
WEB.18: The construction of its wall was jasper. The city was pure gold, like pure glass.
WEB.19: The foundations of the city’s wall were adorned with all kinds of precious stones. The first foundation was jasper; the second, sapphire;  the third, chalcedony; the fourth, emerald;
WEB.20: the fifth, sardonyx; the sixth, sardius; the seventh, chrysolite; the eighth, beryl; the ninth, topaz; the tenth, chrysoprasus; the eleventh, jacinth; and the twelfth, amethyst.
WEB.21: The twelve gates were twelve pearls. Each one of the gates was made of one pearl. The street of the city was pure gold, like transparent glass.
WEB.22: I saw no temple in it, for the Lord God, the Almighty, and the Lamb, are its temple.
WEB.23: The city has no need for the sun, neither of the moon, to shine, for the very glory of God illuminated it, and its lamp is the Lamb.
WEB.24: The nations will walk in its light. The kings of the earth bring the glory and honor of the nations into it.
WEB.25: Its gates will in no way be shut by day (for there will be no night there),
WEB.26: and they shall bring the glory and the honor of the nations into it so that they may enter.
WEB.27: There will in no way enter into it anything profane, or one who causes an abomination or a lie, but only those who are written in the Lamb’s book of life.

CSB17.1: Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth; <sup>ⓓ</sup> for the first heaven and the first earth had passed away, <sup>ⓔ</sup> and the sea was no more. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.2: I also saw the holy city, <sup>ⓖ</sup> the new Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, <sup>ⓗ</sup> prepared like a bride adorned for her husband. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.3: Then I heard a loud voice <sup>ⓙ</sup> from the throne: <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓚ</sup> Look, God’s dwelling <sup>[2]</sup> is with humanity, and he will live with them. They will be his peoples, <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓛ</sup> and God himself will be with them and will be their God. <sup>[4]</sup>
CSB17.4: He will wipe away every tear from their eyes. <sup>ⓜ</sup> Death will be no more; <sup>ⓝ</sup> grief, crying, and pain will be no more, <sup>ⓐ</sup> because the previous things <sup>[5]</sup> have passed away. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.5: Then the one seated on the throne said, “Look, I am making everything new.” He also said, “Write, because these words <sup>[6]</sup> are faithful and true.” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.6: Then he said to me, “It is done! <sup>ⓓ</sup> I am the Alpha and the Omega, <sup>ⓔ</sup> the beginning and the end. <sup>ⓕ</sup> I will freely give to the thirsty <sup>ⓖ</sup> from the spring of the water of life. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.7: The one who conquers <sup>ⓘ</sup> will inherit these things, and I will be his God, and he will be my son. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.8: But the cowards, faithless, <sup>[7]</sup> detestable, murderers, sexually immoral, sorcerers, idolaters, and all liars <sup>ⓚ</sup>  — their share will be in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur, <sup>ⓛ</sup> which is the second death.” <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.9: Then one of the seven angels, who had held the seven bowls filled with the seven last plagues, <sup>ⓝ</sup> came and spoke with me: “Come, I will show you the bride, the wife of the Lamb.”
CSB17.10: He then carried me away in the Spirit <sup>[8]</sup> <sup>ⓐ</sup> to a great, high mountain <sup>ⓑ</sup> and showed me the holy city, Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God,
CSB17.11: arrayed with God’s glory. <sup>ⓒ</sup> Her radiance was like a precious jewel, like a jasper stone, clear as crystal.
CSB17.12: The city had a massive high wall, with twelve gates. Twelve angels were at the gates; the names of the twelve tribes of Israel’s sons were inscribed on the gates.
CSB17.13: There were three gates on the east, three gates on the north, three gates on the south, and three gates on the west. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.14: The city wall had twelve foundations, and the twelve names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb were on the foundations.
CSB17.15: The one who spoke with me had a golden measuring rod <sup>ⓔ</sup> to measure the city, its gates, and its wall. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.16: The city is laid out in a square; its length and width are the same. He measured the city with the rod at 12,000 stadia. [9] Its length, width, and height are equal.
CSB17.17: Then he measured its wall, 144 cubits according to human measurement, which the angel used.
CSB17.18: The building material of its wall was jasper, and the city was pure gold clear as glass.
CSB17.19: The foundations of the city wall were adorned with every kind of jewel: <sup>ⓖ</sup> the first foundation is jasper, the second sapphire, the third chalcedony, the fourth emerald,
CSB17.20: the fifth sardonyx, the sixth carnelian, the seventh chrysolite, the eighth beryl, the ninth topaz, the tenth chrysoprase, the eleventh jacinth, the twelfth amethyst.
CSB17.21: The twelve gates are twelve pearls; each individual gate was made of a single pearl. The main street <sup>[10]</sup> of the city was pure gold, transparent as glass.
CSB17.22: I did not see a temple in it, because the Lord God the Almighty and the Lamb are its temple.
CSB17.23: The city does not need the sun or the moon to shine on it, because the glory of God illuminates it, and its lamp is the Lamb. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.24: The nations <sup>[11]</sup> will walk by its light, <sup>ⓘ</sup> and the kings of the earth will bring their glory into it. <sup>[12]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.25: Its gates will never close by day because it will never be night there. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.26: They will bring the glory and honor of the nations into it. <sup>[13]</sup> <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.27: Nothing unclean will ever enter it, <sup>ⓜ</sup> nor anyone who does what is detestable or false, but only those written in the Lamb’s book of life. <sup>ⓝ</sup>

AMP.1: Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth; for the first heaven and the first earth had passed away (vanished), and there is no longer any sea.
AMP.2: And I saw the holy city, new Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, arrayed like a bride adorned for her husband;
AMP.3: and then I heard a loud voice from the throne, saying, "See! The tabernacle of God is among men, and He will live among them, and they will be His people, and God Himself will be with them[as their God,]
AMP.4: and He will wipe away every tear from their eyes; and there will no longer be death; there will no longer be sorrow and anguish, or crying, or pain; for the former order of things has passed away."
AMP.5: And He who sits on the throne said, "Behold, I am making all things new." Also He said, "Write, for these words are faithful and true [they are accurate, incorruptible, and trustworthy]."
AMP.6: And He said to me, "It is done. I am the Alpha and the Omega, the Beginning and the End. To the one who thirsts I will give [water] from the fountain of the water of life without cost.
AMP.7: He who overcomes [the world by adhering faithfully to Christ Jesus as Lord and Savior] will inherit these things, and I will be his God and he will be My son.
AMP.8: But as for the cowards and unbelieving and abominable [who are devoid of character and personal integrity and practice or tolerate immorality], and murderers, and sorcerers [with intoxicating drugs], and idolaters and occultists [who practice and teach false religions], and all the liars [who knowingly deceive and twist truth], their part will be in the lake that blazes with fire and brimstone, which is the second death."
AMP.9: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls filled with the seven final plagues came and spoke with me, saying, "Come here, I will show you the bride, the wife of the Lamb."
AMP.10: And he carried me away in the Spirit to a vast and lofty mountain, and showed me the holy (sanctified) city of Jerusalem coming down out of heaven from God,
AMP.11: having God's glory [filled with His radiant light]. The brilliance of it resembled a rare and very precious jewel, like jasper, shining and clear as crystal.
AMP.12: It had a massive and high wall, with twelve [large] gates, and at the gates [were stationed] twelve angels; and on the gates the names of the twelve tribes of the sons of Israel were written.
AMP.13: On the east side [there were] three gates, on the north three gates, on the south three gates, and on the west three gates.
AMP.14: And the wall of the city had twelve foundation stones, and on them the twelve names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb (Christ).
AMP.15: The one who was speaking with me had a gold measuring rod to measure the city, and its gates and its wall.
AMP.16: The city is laid out as a square, its length being the same as its width; and he measured the city with his rod—twelve thousand stadia (about 1,400 miles); its length and width and height are equal.
AMP.17: He measured its wall also—a hundred forty-four cubits (about 200 feet), according to man's measurements, which are [also] angelic [measurements].
AMP.18: The wall was built of jasper; and the city was pure gold, transparent like clear crystal.
AMP.19: The foundation stones of the wall of the city were adorned with every kind of precious stone. The first foundation stone was jasper; the second, sapphire; the third, chalcedony; the fourth, emerald;
AMP.20: the fifth, sardonyx; the sixth, sardius; the seventh, chrysolite (yellow topaz); the eighth, beryl; the ninth, topaz; the tenth, chrysoprase; the eleventh, jacinth; the twelfth, amethyst.
AMP.21: And the twelve gates were twelve pearls; each separate gate was of one single pearl. And the street (broad way) of the city was pure gold, like transparent crystal.
AMP.22: I saw no temple in it, for the Lord God Almighty [the Omnipotent, the Ruler of all] and the Lamb are its temple.
AMP.23: And the city has no need of the sun nor of the moon to give light to it, for the glory (splendor, radiance) of God has illumined it, and the Lamb is its lamp and light.
AMP.24: The nations [the redeemed people from the earth] will walk by its light, and the kings of the earth will bring into it their glory.
AMP.25: By day (for there will be no night there) its gates will never be closed [in fear of evil];
AMP.26: and they will bring the glory (splendor, majesty) and the honor of the nations into it;
AMP.27: and nothing that defiles or profanes or is unwashed will ever enter it, nor anyone who practices abominations [detestable, morally repugnant things] and lying, but only those [will be admitted] whose names have been written in the Lamb's Book of Life.

MSG.1: I saw Heaven and earth new-created. Gone the first Heaven, gone the first earth, gone the sea.
MSG.2: I saw Holy Jerusalem, new-created, descending resplendent out of Heaven, as ready for God as a bride for her husband.
MSG.3: I heard a voice thunder from the Throne: "Look! Look! God has moved into the neighborhood, making his home with men and women! They're his people, he's their God.
MSG.4: He'll wipe every tear from their eyes. Death is gone for good—tears gone, crying gone, pain gone—all the first order of things gone."
MSG.5: The Enthroned continued, "Look! I'm making everything new. Write it all down—each word dependable and accurate."
MSG.6: Then he said, "It's happened. I'm A to Z. I'm the Beginning, I'm the Conclusion. From Water-of-Life Well I give freely to the thirsty.
MSG.7: Conquerors inherit all this. I'll be God to them, they'll be sons and daughters to me.
MSG.8: But for the rest—the feckless and faithless, degenerates and murderers, sex peddlers and sorcerers, idolaters and all liars—for them it's Lake Fire and Brimstone. Second death!"
MSG.9: One of the Seven Angels who had carried the bowls filled with the seven final disasters spoke to me: "Come here. I'll show you the Bride, the Wife of the Lamb."
MSG.10: He took me away in the Spirit to an enormous, high mountain and showed me Holy Jerusalem descending out of Heaven from God,
MSG.11: resplendent in the bright glory of God. The City shimmered like a precious gem, light-filled, pulsing light.
MSG.12: She had a wall majestic and high with twelve gates. At each gate stood an Angel, and on the gates were inscribed the names of the Twelve Tribes of the sons of Israel:
MSG.13: three gates on the east, three gates on the north, three gates on the south, three gates on the west.
MSG.14: The wall was set on twelve foundations, the names of the Twelve Apostles of the Lamb inscribed on them.
MSG.15: The Angel speaking with me had a gold measuring stick to measure the City, its gates, and its wall.
MSG.16: The City was laid out in a perfect square. He measured the City with the measuring stick: twelve thousand stadia, its length, width, and height all equal.
MSG.17: Using the standard measure, the Angel measured the thickness of its wall: 144 cubits.
MSG.18: The wall was jasper, the color of Glory, and the City was pure gold, translucent as glass.
MSG.19: The foundations of the City walls were garnished with every precious gem imaginable: the first foundation jasper, the second sapphire, the third agate, the fourth emerald,
MSG.20: the fifth onyx, the sixth carnelian, the seventh chrysolite, the eighth beryl, the ninth topaz, the tenth chrysoprase, the eleventh jacinth, the twelfth amethyst.
MSG.21: The twelve gates were twelve pearls, each gate a single pearl. The main street of the City was pure gold, translucent as glass.
MSG.22: But there was no sign of a Temple, for the Lord God—the Sovereign-Strong—and the Lamb are the Temple.
MSG.23: The City doesn't need sun or moon for light. God's Glory is its light, the Lamb its lamp!
MSG.24: The nations will walk in its light and earth's kings bring in their splendor.
MSG.25: Its gates will never be shut by day, and there won't be any night.
MSG.26: They'll bring the glory and honor of the nations into the City.
MSG.27: Nothing dirty or defiled will get into the City, and no one who defiles or deceives. Only those whose names are written in the Lamb's Book of Life will get in.

NIV.1:
The New Jerusalem
Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth, for the first heaven and the first earth had passed away, and there was no longer any sea.
NIV.2: I saw the Holy City, the new Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, prepared as a bride beautifully dressed for her husband.
NIV.3: And I heard a loud voice from the throne saying, “Now the dwelling of God is with men, and he will live with them. They will be his people, and God himself will be with them and be their God.
NIV.4: He will wipe every tear from their eyes. There will be no more death or mourning or crying or pain, for the old order of things has passed away.”
NIV.5: He who was seated on the throne said, “I am making everything new!” Then he said, “Write this down, for these words are trustworthy and true.”
NIV.6: He said to me: “It is done. I am the Alpha and the Omega, the Beginning and the End. To him who is thirsty I will give to drink without cost from the spring of the water of life.
NIV.7: He who overcomes will inherit all this, and I will be his God and he will be my son.
NIV.8: But the cowardly, the unbelieving, the vile, the murderers, the sexually immoral, those who practice magic arts, the idolaters and all liars—their place will be in the fiery lake of burning sulfur. This is the second death.”
NIV.9: One of the seven angels who had the seven bowls full of the seven last plagues came and said to me, “Come, I will show you the bride, the wife of the Lamb.”
NIV.10: And he carried me away in the Spirit to a mountain great and high, and showed me the Holy City, Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God.
NIV.11: It shone with the glory of God, and its brilliance was like that of a very precious jewel, like a jasper, clear as crystal.
NIV.12: It had a great, high wall with twelve gates, and with twelve angels at the gates. On the gates were written the names of the twelve tribes of Israel.
NIV.13: There were three gates on the east, three on the north, three on the south and three on the west.
NIV.14: The wall of the city had twelve foundations, and on them were the names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.
NIV.15: The angel who talked with me had a measuring rod of gold to measure the city, its gates and its walls.
NIV.16: The city was laid out like a square, as long as it was wide. He measured the city with the rod and found it to be 12,000 stadia in length, and as wide and high as it is long.
NIV.17: He measured its wall and it was 144 cubits thick, by man’s measurement, which the angel was using.
NIV.18: The wall was made of jasper, and the city of pure gold, as pure as glass.
NIV.19: The foundations of the city walls were decorated with every kind of precious stone. The first foundation was jasper, the second sapphire, the third chalcedony, the fourth emerald,
NIV.20: the fifth sardonyx, the sixth carnelian, the seventh chrysolite, the eighth beryl, the ninth topaz, the tenth chrysoprase, the eleventh jacinth, and the twelfth amethyst.
NIV.21: The twelve gates were twelve pearls, each gate made of a single pearl. The great street of the city was of pure gold, like transparent glass.
NIV.22: I did not see a temple in the city, because the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb are its temple.
NIV.23: The city does not need the sun or the moon to shine on it, for the glory of God gives it light, and the Lamb is its lamp.
NIV.24: The nations will walk by its light, and the kings of the earth will bring their splendor into it.
NIV.25: On no day will its gates ever be shut, for there will be no night there.
NIV.26: The glory and honor of the nations will be brought into it.
NIV.27: Nothing impure will ever enter it, nor will anyone who does what is shameful or deceitful, but only those whose names are written in the Lamb’s book of life.

NRSVCE.1: Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth; for the first heaven and the first earth had passed away, and the sea was no more.
NRSVCE.2: And I saw the holy city, the new Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, prepared as a bride adorned for her husband.
NRSVCE.3:
And I heard a loud voice from the throne saying, “See, the home of God is among mortals.
He will dwell with them;
they will be his peoples,
and God himself will be with them;
NRSVCE.4:
he will wipe every tear from their eyes.
Death will be no more;
mourning and crying and pain will be no more,
for the first things have passed away.”
NRSVCE.5: And the one who was seated on the throne said, “See, I am making all things new.” Also he said, “Write this, for these words are trustworthy and true.”
NRSVCE.6: Then he said to me, “It is done! I am the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. To the thirsty I will give water as a gift from the spring of the water of life.
NRSVCE.7: Those who conquer will inherit these things, and I will be their God and they will be my children.
NRSVCE.8: But as for the cowardly, the faithless, the polluted, the murderers, the fornicators, the sorcerers, the idolaters, and all liars, their place will be in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur, which is the second death.”
NRSVCE.9: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls full of the seven last plagues came and said to me, “Come, I will show you the bride, the wife of the Lamb.”
NRSVCE.10: And in the spirit he carried me away to a great, high mountain and showed me the holy city Jerusalem coming down out of heaven from God.
NRSVCE.11: It has the glory of God and a radiance like a very rare jewel, like jasper, clear as crystal.
NRSVCE.12: It has a great, high wall with twelve gates, and at the gates twelve angels, and on the gates are inscribed the names of the twelve tribes of the Israelites;
NRSVCE.13: on the east three gates, on the north three gates, on the south three gates, and on the west three gates.
NRSVCE.14: And the wall of the city has twelve foundations, and on them are the twelve names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.
NRSVCE.15: The angel who talked to me had a measuring rod of gold to measure the city and its gates and walls.
NRSVCE.16: The city lies foursquare, its length the same as its width; and he measured the city with his rod, fifteen hundred miles; its length and width and height are equal.
NRSVCE.17: He also measured its wall, one hundred forty-four cubits by human measurement, which the angel was using.
NRSVCE.18: The wall is built of jasper, while the city is pure gold, clear as glass.
NRSVCE.19: The foundations of the wall of the city are adorned with every jewel; the first was jasper, the second sapphire, the third agate, the fourth emerald,
NRSVCE.20: the fifth onyx, the sixth carnelian, the seventh chrysolite, the eighth beryl, the ninth topaz, the tenth chrysoprase, the eleventh jacinth, the twelfth amethyst.
NRSVCE.21: And the twelve gates are twelve pearls, each of the gates is a single pearl, and the street of the city is pure gold, transparent as glass.
NRSVCE.22: I saw no temple in the city, for its temple is the Lord God the Almighty and the Lamb.
NRSVCE.23: And the city has no need of sun or moon to shine on it, for the glory of God is its light, and its lamp is the Lamb.
NRSVCE.24: The nations will walk by its light, and the kings of the earth will bring their glory into it.
NRSVCE.25: Its gates will never be shut by day — and there will be no night there.
NRSVCE.26: People will bring into it the glory and the honor of the nations.
NRSVCE.27: But nothing unclean will enter it, nor anyone who practices abomination or falsehood, but only those who are written in the Lamb’s book of life.

NET.1: Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth, for the first heaven and earth had ceased to exist, and the sea existed no more.
NET.2: And I saw the holy city — the new Jerusalem — descending out of heaven from God, made ready like a bride adorned for her husband.
NET.3: And I heard a loud voice from the throne saying: “Look! The residence of God is among human beings. He will live among them, and they will be his people, and God himself will be with them.
NET.4: He will wipe away every tear from their eyes, and death will not exist any more — or mourning, or crying, or pain, for the former things have ceased to exist.”
NET.5: And the one seated on the throne said: “Look! I am making all things new!” Then he said to me, “Write it down, because these words are reliable and true.”
NET.6: He also said to me, “It is done! I am the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. To the one who is thirsty I will give water free of charge from the spring of the water of life.
NET.7: The one who conquers will inherit these things, and I will be his God and he will be my son.
NET.8: But to the cowards, unbelievers, detestable persons, murderers, the sexually immoral, and those who practice magic spells, idol worshipers, and all those who lie, their place will be in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur. That is the second death.”
NET.9: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls full of the seven final plagues came and spoke to me, saying, “Come, I will show you the bride, the wife of the Lamb!”
NET.10: So he took me away in the Spirit to a huge, majestic mountain and showed me the holy city, Jerusalem, descending out of heaven from God.
NET.11: The city possesses the glory of God; its brilliance is like a precious jewel, like a stone of crystal-clear jasper.
NET.12: It has a massive, high wall with twelve gates, with twelve angels at the gates, and the names of the twelve tribes of the nation of Israel are written on the gates.
NET.13: There are three gates on the east side, three gates on the north side, three gates on the south side and three gates on the west side.
NET.14: The wall of the city has twelve foundations, and on them are the twelve names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.
NET.15: The angel who spoke to me had a golden measuring rod with which to measure the city and its foundation stones and wall.
NET.16: Now the city is laid out as a square, its length and width the same. He measured the city with the measuring rod at fourteen hundred miles (its length and width and height are equal).
NET.17: He also measured its wall, one hundred forty-four cubits according to human measurement, which is also the angel’s.
NET.18: The city’s wall is made of jasper and the city is pure gold, like transparent glass.
NET.19: The foundations of the city’s wall are decorated with every kind of precious stone. The first foundation is jasper, the second sapphire, the third agate, the fourth emerald,
NET.20: the fifth onyx, the sixth carnelian, the seventh chrysolite, the eighth beryl, the ninth topaz, the tenth chrysoprase, the eleventh jacinth, and the twelfth amethyst.
NET.21: And the twelve gates are twelve pearls — each one of the gates is made from just one pearl! The main street of the city is pure gold, like transparent glass.
NET.22: Now I saw no temple in the city, because the Lord God — the All-Powerful — and the Lamb are its temple.
NET.23: The city does not need the sun or the moon to shine on it, because the glory of God lights it up, and its lamp is the Lamb.
NET.24: The nations will walk by its light and the kings of the earth will bring their grandeur into it.
NET.25: Its gates will never be closed during the day (and there will be no night there).
NET.26: They will bring the grandeur and the wealth of the nations into it,
NET.27: but nothing ritually unclean will ever enter into it, nor anyone who does what is detestable or practices falsehood, but only those whose names are written in the Lamb’s book of life.
